proven.claim: PROVE: the threshold is applied exactly — referencedVars >= 2 iff contributes=1.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 93
; PROVE: the threshold is applied exactly — referencedVars >= 2 iff contributes=1.
; Models the conditional: if (referencedVars >= 2) transitionCount++
(declare-const refs_in_assert Int)    ; referencedVars for one assertion
(declare-const contributes Int)       ; 1 if this assert increments transitionCount
; Code: the conditional check
(assert (=> (>= refs_in_assert 2) (= contributes 1)))
(assert (=> (< refs_in_assert 2) (= contributes 0)))
(assert (>= refs_in_assert 0))
; Negate: refs >= 2 but contributes = 0 (threshold not applied)
(assert (>= refs_in_assert 2))
(assert (= contributes 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: boundary at referencedVars = 1 — genuine single-variable transitions are excluded.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 93
; VIOLATION: boundary at referencedVars = 1 — genuine single-variable transitions are excluded.
; A block with N assertions each referencing exactly 1 variable stays at transitionCount=0
; regardless of whether those assertions encode meaningful state constraints.
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const single_var_count Int)   ; asserts where referencedVars = 1 exactly
(declare-const transition_count Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Multiple assertions, each referencing exactly one declared variable
(assert (> num_asserts 1))
(assert (= single_var_count num_asserts))
; Code: partition — all assertions fall below the threshold
(assert (= (+ single_var_count transition_count) num_asserts))
(assert (= transition_count 0))
; Code: return true (vacuous)
(assert (= result 1))
; Violation: a block like:
;   (assert (>= balance 0))
;   (assert (= balance_after (- balance 100)))  ← actually 2 vars, counted
; versus:
;   (assert (>= x 0))
;   (assert (< x 100))
; The second block has x in both asserts but referencedVars=1 per assert →
; transitionCount=0 → classified vacuous despite meaningful constraints
(check-sat)
